title: Kamino uprising

{{Quote|Officially, there never was a 'Clone rebellion' on Kamino. Unofficially, approximately twenty years after we were created, a special detachment of the Imperial 501st Legion was dispatched to Kamino with orders to eradicate an army of clones that had been bred to take arms against the Empire. Our mission commander, an expert on the inner workings of Kamino, was a young bounty hunter named Boba Fett.|Entry from the [[501st Journal]]|<ref name="BFII" />}}
The '''Kamino uprising''' was a conflict in [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]] on the aquatic planet [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]], the origin world of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] during the waning days of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]]. After having spent the better part of the last couple decades growing [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] for the Old Republic and [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] under [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], a group of Kaminoans went rogue, using their cloning resources to grow an army with the intent of combating Palpatine's [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. The Kaminoan resistance once again used the Grand Army of the Republic's template, [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett's]] DNA and equipped their soldiers with [[Phase I clone trooper armor/Legends|phase I clone trooper armor]].

Upon learning of the insurrection, the Galactic Empire hired renowned bounty hunter [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], himself a clone of Jango Fett, to penetrate and sabotage the cloning facilities of [[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]]. Fett's childhood spent in the city granted him intricate knowledge of the facility's layout, and he led the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] to a crushing defeat of the "anti-clone" army. The Kaminoan leadership themselves were destroyed when their [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|escape transports]] were shot down as they attempted to flee. Before the battle's end, Fett extracted a sample of the Jango Fett DNA and delivered it to his Imperial clients. The uprising led to a restructuring of the Imperial cloning policy; the previous model of centralized cloning sources was abandoned in favor of having a wide variety of cloning centers and genetic templates.

==Prelude==
{{Quote|So Palpatine has a new army. I have no doubt he'll find our cloning operations a threat one day soon, and seek to destroy our capabilities. But he's a fool if he believes we handed over all the combat-trained Fett clones to him.|Lama Su, Kaminoan prime minister, on discovering Palpatine's new Imperial Army|<ref name="Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref>}}
In the years following the rise of the Empire, the planet [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] was kept under the watchful eye of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], in order to ensure that the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] [[Cloning/Legends|cloning]] technology remained under Imperial control. A small group of [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoans]] known as [[Clone Master/Legends|Clone Masters]], resentful of the Empire's presence, began to grow an army of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] to use in order to fight against their oppressors. These rogue clone troopers were called [[Anti-trooper|clone anti-Imperial troopers]] because they were created to fight against the Empire. They wore Phase I [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone trooper]] armor and were mostly [[Clone jet trooper/Legends|Jet Troopers]] and [[Advanced Recon Commando heavy gunner|Rail ARC Commandos]]. The conflict pitted these Kamino-grown troops against their own Imperial brothers: members of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], who were led by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], since he had been "born" and raised on Kamino and had a great knowledge of the facility.<ref name="BFII" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|Remember leading an Imperial force against clone troops on Kamino? You didn't feel that we were family then.|[[Jaing Skirata]], to [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]|<ref name="Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref>}}
[[File:BF2_Kamino_2.jpg|thumb|left|220px|An Imperial [[Phase Zero dark trooper|dark trooper]] attacks Kaminoian clone troopers.]]
The Imperial forces landed in the city in a [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|''Sentinel''-class landing craft]]. Boba Fett, hired for his knowledge of the cloning facility, made his way towards the cloning facility and destroyed a door control console that kept the facility in lockdown and proceeded inside, killing any rogue clones who got in his way. Fett and his troops were then ordered to retrieve a Template Sample of Jango Fett's [[DNA/Legends|DNA]] to prevent the Kaminoans from using it to clone more rogue soldiers. After getting his hands on the sample, Fett returned it to the Landing Craft which shortly took off upon acquiring it from Fett. The bounty hunter then returned to the facility and wiped out the life support systems located in the lower cloning center, ending any chances of more anti-Imperial clones being grown. Meanwhile, his fellow Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] fought the enemy clones on the numerous platforms of [[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]].<ref name="BFII" />

[[File:BF2_Kamino_3.jpg|thumb|right|220px|[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] destroys a Kaminoian turret.]]
After Fett finished his work in the cloning facilities, he met up with the troopers outside and aided them in the battle. Mere moments after the clone life support was destroyed, the last of the anti-Imperial troopers were pushed back onto the landing platforms and finally destroyed. The leaders of the Kaminoan rebels attempted to escape in two [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/is]], but were brought down by rockets fired from [[Imperial shock trooper/Legends|Imperial shock troopers]] and from lasers fired by ground turrets.<ref name="BFII" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|The bounty hunter left after the battle was over, he said something about tracking down a smuggler on Tatooine. After the Kamino uprising, the Emperor decided that an army of genetically identical soldiers was too susceptible to corruption. Future troopers would be cloned from a variety of templates. Though the 501st itself remained pure, the rest of the Imperial Army gradually became more and more diverse. We never really got used to the new guys.|Entry from the 501st Journal|<ref name="BFII" />}}
With the traitorous clones exterminated, Kamino remained under Imperial control and the rogue cloning facilities were commandeered unto Imperial rule. The surviving leaders of the Kaminoan resistance were arrested and detained onboard the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. Most would join [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel]] inmates and die fighting during the [[Death Star Uprising]].

Although the clone rebellion was soundly defeated, this incident convinced [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] to commission the creation of new clones from different templates and the recruitment of birth-born soldiers to fill the ranks of the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]]. He also did this because he believed that the non birth-born troopers were too susceptible to corruption, and believed that a more diverse group of soldiers would solve this issue. Although the Jango Fett template would still be used, none of the Fett clones, especially the members of the 501st who defeated the rebel clones, would ever get used to the increasing diversity in the army ranks that they once filled as a whole.<ref name="BFII" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Kamino uprising first appeared in the [[2005]] [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' as the fourth mission in the Empire section of the "Rise of the Empire" campaign. The event was later mentioned a few times during the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' novel series.
